engineGuide.h2: Ford F-550 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Ford F-550 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 7L Diesel
entries.history: Ford's first in-house diesel, code-named "Scorpion", introduced in 2011 to replace the problematic 6.4L. Entirely Ford-designed with AVL of Austria.
compatibleVariants: XL, XLT, Lariat, Limited, Chassis Cab, Cutaway, Crew Cab, Regular Cab, 4x4, 4x2, Ambulance Prep, Motorhome Conversion
commonFailures: CP4 fuel pump failure (2011-2014 models) - catastrophic fuel system contamination
EGR cooler clogging leading to reduced performance
entries.cta: Get quotes for SCAT Ford F-550 engine replacement

entries.title: 4L Diesel
entries.history: Short-lived Power Stroke produced from 2008-2010, known for twin sequential turbos but plagued by reliability issues that drove high replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: XL, XLT, Lariat, Chassis Cab, Crew Cab, Regular Cab
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6.4PS Ford F-550 engine replacement

entries.title: 0L Diesel
entries.history: Navistar-built Power Stroke fitted to early F-550 imports, offering 325hp but gaining a reputation for EGR and head gasket vulnerabilities.
compatibleVariants: XL, XLT, Chassis Cab, Crew Cab, Regular Cab
commonFailures: EGR cooler failure leading to head gasket failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6.0PS Ford F-550 engine replacement

entries.title: 2L Petrol
entries.history: Codename "Hurricane" became "Boss" - a cast-iron block V8 with aluminium heads debuting in 2011. Proven reliable with many high-mileage examples still on UK roads.
compatibleVariants: XL, XLT, Lariat, Chassis Cab, Crew Cab, Regular Cab, 4x4, 4x2
commonFailures: Exhaust manifold leaks and broken hardware (cold-start tick)
Rough idle from spark plugs or broken valve springs
IMRC runner flap failure causing knock-like noise
entries.cta: Get quotes for Y1GA Ford F-550 engine replacement

entries.title: 3L Petrol
entries.history: Ford's return to pushrod V8 design for Super Duty trucks. Simple, high-displacement engine prioritising low-end torque and durability for commercial applications.
compatibleVariants: XL, XLT, Lariat, Limited, Chassis Cab, Cutaway, Crew Cab, Regular Cab
commonFailures: Oil pump drive gear cracking (SSM 50830, 2020-2022 models)
Lifter delamination (reported in high-idle-hour fleet vehicles)
Ticking/clicking noise from front timing cover area
entries.cta: Get quotes for Z1GA Ford F-550 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Ford F-550 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: CP4 Fuel Pump Failure - The £10,000+ Catastrophic Failure That Ends 6.7L Power Stroke Engines
problems.affectedModels: Ford F-550 Chassis Cab, Cutaway, Crew Cab, Regular Cab (2011-2014, SCAT engine code / 2015-2016, R2GA engine code with CP4 pump)
problems.rootCause: The Bosch CP4 high-pressure fuel pump uses a roller-and-cam design lubricated by diesel fuel. Low lubricity UK fuel, air ingress, or water contamination causes the pump to self-destruct, sending metal fragments throughout the entire fuel system - injectors, rails, lines, and tank.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative - CP4 Bypass / Disaster Kit - Install pump protection kit before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fitting lift pump or CP4 bypass kit / disaster prevention system to catch debris
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents failure entirely; recommended for any pre-2017 F-550

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Complete Fuel System - After CP4 failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement CP4 pump, 8 injectors, fuel rails, lines, tank clean, filter housing
repairOptions.longevity: Restores engine but original CP4 pump design remains fitted

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Upgraded DCR Pump Conversion - Best long-term solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: S&S DCR pump conversion kit (removes CP4), new injectors, full fuel system replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix; eliminates CP4 risk entirely; 200,000+ mile life

problems.recommendation: For 2011-2016 F-550s still on the original CP4, fit a preventative kit (£800-£1,500) immediately - this is the cheapest insurance you can buy. If your CP4 has already failed, spend the extra £2,000-£3,000 on the DCR pump conversion rather than replacing like-for-like. The CP4 will fail again on a high-mileage commercial truck. On an F-550 worth £15,000-£25,000, a £10,000 DCR conversion keeps the truck on the road for another decade.
problems.cta: Compare CP4 failure repair costs for your Ford F-550 6.7L Power Stroke

problems.h4: EGR Cooler Failure - When Coolant Destroys Your 6.7L Power Stroke
problems.affectedModels: Ford F-550 Chassis Cab, Cutaway, Motorhome Conversion (2011-2019, SCAT & R2GA/R2GB engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Thermal cycling between 1,200°F exhaust gas and 200°F coolant creates metal fatigue in the EGR cooler passages. Cracks develop, allowing coolant to leak into the intake manifold and combustion chambers - leading to hydro-lock, bent connecting rods, and complete engine failure.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Cooler Replacement - Catch it early
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace primary EGR cooler, secondary valve cover radiator, flush cooling system
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000-80,000 miles if done correctly with both coolers

repairOptions.tier: Major - EGR Cooler + Cylinder Head Inspection - For 2011-2012 models
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Both coolers replaced plus cylinder head pressure testing and potential replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Required for 2011-2012 F-550s where 40% have cracked heads

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Complete Engine Rebuild - After hydro-lock
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full engine rebuild with new connecting rods, pistons, and upgraded EGR system
repairOptions.longevity: Only when hydro-lock has bent rods; £12,000+ repair

problems.recommendation: If you see white smoke from the exhaust on cold starts or notice coolant loss with no visible leak, stop driving immediately. Catching EGR cooler failure before hydro-lock turns a £1,500-£3,000 repair into a £15,000 engine rebuild. For 2011-2012 F-550s, always pressure-test cylinder heads during cooler replacement - skipping this £100 step risks a £6,000 head replacement months later.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Ford F-550 6.7L EGR cooler replacement from UK specialists

problems.group: Turbocharger Vane Sticking & Ceramic Bearing Failure
problems.h4: Turbocharger Vane Sticking & Ceramic Bearing Failure - The 2011-2014 Power Stroke Weak Point
problems.affectedModels: Ford F-550 Chassis Cab, Cutaway, Ambulance Prep (2011-2014, SCAT engine code with Honeywell DualBoost turbo)
problems.rootCause: Early Honeywell turbos use ceramic ball bearings that shatter under high RPM loads and thermal cycling. Combined with soot buildup on variable geometry turbo (VGT) vanes from extended idling, the turbo loses boost control, screeches, and eventually fails catastrophically.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - VGT Vane Cleaning - Preventative maintenance
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical cleaning of VGT vanes through intake or turbo removal
Replace with OE-spec turbo (still ceramic bearings on 2011-2014)

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Upgraded Steel Bearing Turbo - Permanent fix
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Upgrade turbo with steel bearings and revised VGT design

problems.recommendation: On a 2011-2014 F-550, don't waste money on an OEM replacement turbo - it still has ceramic bearings and will fail again. Pay the premium for an upgraded steel-bearing turbo (£3,000-£5,000 specialist fitted). The cost difference is £1,000-£1,500 but doubles the lifespan. If you hear a high-pitched screeching noise from the engine bay, stop driving immediately - catastrophic turbo failure can send metal into the intake.
problems.cta: Compare Ford F-550 6.7L turbo replacement prices - upgraded steel bearing options available

problems.group: 6.2L Boss V8 Broken Valve Springs & Exhaust Manifold Leaks
problems.h4: 6.2L Boss V8 Broken Valve Springs & Exhaust Manifold Leaks - The Petrol F-550's Annoying Failure
problems.affectedModels: Ford F-550 XL, XLT, Chassis Cab (2011-2019, Y1GA / Y1GB engine codes)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust manifold bolts corrode and snap due to thermal cycling, creating a ticking noise that worsens over time. Broken valve springs (usually due to fatigue or poor maintenance) cause rough idle, misfire, and potential piston-to-valve contact. The 6.2L is otherwise durable, but neglect accelerates these failures .

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Exhaust Manifold Gasket & Bolt Replacement - Fix ticking
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace snapped manifold bolts, new gaskets, sometimes manifold resurfacing

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Valve Spring Replacement - Rough idle / misfire fix
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace broken valve springs (typically 1-2 cylinders), valve clearance check
repairOptions.longevity: Restores engine; inspect all springs while in there

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 6.2L Engine - After catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt long block; supply only

problems.recommendation: The 6.2L Boss is fundamentally reliable - most failures come from neglect, not design flaws . If you hear a ticking noise from the exhaust that disappears when warm, fix manifold bolts early (£450-£800) before the manifold warps. For rough idle or misfire, diagnose valve springs before assuming injector failure. A rebuilt 6.2L costs £2,800-£5,200 supply only - excellent value for keeping a petrol F-550 on the road.
problems.cta: Compare Ford F-550 6.2L Boss V8 engine prices - rebuilt units available nationwide

problems.h4: 7.3L Godzilla V8 Camshaft & Lifter Wear - Low RPM Idling Destroys This Engine
problems.affectedModels: Ford F-550 XL, XLT, Lariat, Chassis Cab (2020-Present, Z1GA / Z1GB engine codes)
problems.rootCause: The camshaft is the last component in the oil circuit. Extended low-RPM idling (common in delivery and fleet trucks) reduces oil pressure and flow to the camshaft and lifters. Combined with thin recommended oils, this causes lifter roller bearing failure and cam lobe wear. Rental and fleet vehicles are most affected .

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil Pump Upgrade & Idle Reduction - Preventative
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: High-volume oil pump upgrade; install idle timer or avoid extended idling
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents failure; essential for fleet / high-idle vehicles

repairOptions.tier: Major - Camshaft & Lifter Replacement - After wear detected
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace camshaft, all lifters, oil pump, and flush engine
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000-100,000 miles if root cause (low idle) not addressed

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt 7.3L Engine - After catastrophic cam/lifter failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt long block; includes upgraded oil pump
repairOptions.longevity: 150,000+ miles with upgraded pump and idle management

problems.recommendation: If your F-550 7.3L Godzilla idles for long periods (delivery, ambulance, or fleet use), fit a high-volume oil pump (£400-£800 specialist fitted) and avoid extended idling. The engine simply isn't designed for hours of low-RPM operation with thin oil. If you detect a ticking noise from the valvetrain, diagnose immediately - a £2,500-£4,000 cam and lifter job beats a £5,500-£9,500 engine replacement. This is not a design flaw for normal driving; it's a use-case mismatch for high-idle commercial operation .
problems.cta: Get Ford F-550 7.3L Godzilla engine quotes - specify your operating pattern for accurate pricing

problems.h4: DPF/SCR System Clogging & P20EE Code - The Emissions Nightmare for UK Operators
problems.affectedModels: Ford F-550 6.7L Power Stroke diesel (2011-Present, all SCAT, R2GA, R2GB, R2GC codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 100,000-150,000 miles for DPF; 60,000-100,000 for EGR valve
problems.rootCause: The P20EE code indicates "SCR NOx Catalyst Efficiency Below Threshold" - caused by DPF clogging, DEF injector crystallisation, or EGR valve sticking . UK F-550s used for short journeys or stop-start work never reach regeneration temperatures, causing rapid soot buildup and emissions system failure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - DPF/EGR Professional Cleaning - Catch it early
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Forced DPF regeneration, EGR valve and cooler chemical cleaning
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function for 20,000-40,000 miles if driving habits unchanged

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DEF Injector Replacement - P20EE specific fix
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace DEF injector, test SCR efficiency, clear adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles; often the sole cause of P20EE

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - DPF / SCR System Replacement - After catastrophic clog
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New DPF filter, SCR catalyst, NOx sensors, and DEF injector
repairOptions.longevity: 100,000+ miles; expensive but restores full emissions compliance

problems.recommendation: For UK F-550s operating urban or short routes, budget for £400-£600 professional DPF cleaning every 20,000-30,000 miles. Ignoring the P20EE code leads to limp mode and MOT failure. The cheapest fix is often just the DEF injector (£400-£800) - many specialists replace this first before condemning the entire SCR system. Never delete emissions components on a UK vehicle - it's illegal for road use and will fail MOT.
problems.cta: Compare Ford F-550 DPF cleaning and emissions system repair costs from UK specialists

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Ford F-550
finalCta.paragraph: The failures above account for over 80% of engine replacement enquiries on imported Ford F-550s in the UK. The 6.7L Power Stroke's CP4 pump and EGR cooler are the biggest threats - both have proven preventative solutions that cost a fraction of a full rebuild. Petrol 6.2L and 7.3L F-550s are fundamentally reliable but punish neglect and extended idling.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Ford F-550 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Ford F-550 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Ford F-550 Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Ford F-550 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Dismantled, inspected, worn components replaced to manufacturer tolerances. Not fully remanufactured but rigorously tested.
types.frontDescription: Dismantled, inspected, worn components replaced to manufacturer tolerances. Not fully remanufactured but rigorously tested.
types.frontDisclaimer: Best balance of cost and reliability for high-mileage 6.7L Power Stroke.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned F-550 engines have failed parts replaced but reuse serviceable components like pistons and crankshaft after inspection.
backBullets: Typically 12-24 month warranty on 6.7L Power Stroke SCAT & R2GA units
Ideal for high-value motorhome conversions on a mid-range budget
UK specialists bench-test before delivery to verify compression and oil pressure
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Ford F-550 engine prices

types.title: Ford F-550 Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Full strip-down, hot-tank cleaning, new piston rings, bearings, gaskets, oil pump and timing components.
types.frontDescription: Full strip-down, hot-tank cleaning, new piston rings, bearings, gaskets, oil pump and timing components.
types.frontDisclaimer: Best value for 6.7L Power Stroke with CP4 failure or hydro-lock damage.
types.backDescription: A rebuilt F-550 engine restores compression and oil pressure to factory specifications by renewing all wear components.
backBullets: All 6.7L units receive upgraded CP4 disaster prevention parts as standard
12-month unlimited mileage warranty with option to extend to 24 months
Rebuilt 6.2L Boss and 7.3L Godzilla benefit from upgraded valve springs and oil pumps
types.cta: Get rebuilt Ford F-550 engine quotes

types.title: Ford F-550 Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage salvage units removed from UK-imported donor trucks. Minimal reconditioning beyond compression testing.
types.frontDescription: Low-mileage salvage units removed from UK-imported donor trucks. Minimal reconditioning beyond compression testing.
types.frontDisclaimer: Short-term fix for budget-sensitive commercial operators or test-fit projects.
types.backDescription: Used F-550 engines are the cheapest option but carry higher risk - we vet all suppliers for legitimate mileage and UK import history.
backBullets: 30-90 day warranty typical; read terms carefully for commercial use exclusions
Best suited for 2015-2019 R2GA 6.7L units where CP4 pump already upgraded
Not recommended for 2011-2014 SCAT engines with original CP4 pump fitted
types.cta: Check used Ford F-550 engine availability

types.title: Ford F-550 Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Factory-spec remanufacturing with all components replaced or machined to OEM blueprints. Includes upgraded failure-prone parts.

engineSizes.h2: Ford F-550 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Ford F-550 across all generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, years of production, power outputs, and common failure notes.

items.title: Ford F-550 6.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 6.7L Power Stroke V8 diesel is the dominant engine in UK-imported F-550 chassis cabs, cutaways, and motorhome conversions. Introduced in 2011 as Ford's first in-house diesel design (codenamed "Scorpion"), it replaced the short-lived 6.4L and has undergone four major revisions - SCAT (2011-2014), R2GA (2015-2019), R2GB (2020-2022), and R2GC (2023+). The 6.7L produces between 300hp and 475hp depending on model year and tune, with torque figures ranging from 660lb-ft to over 1,050lb-ft .
commonFailurePoints: CP4 fuel pump failure on 2011-2014 SCAT engines; EGR cooler cracking leading to hydro-lock; turbocharger vane sticking
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford F-550 6.7L diesel engines

items.title: Ford F-550 6.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 6.4L Power Stroke V8 was produced from 2008 to 2010 as a short-lived replacement for the problematic 6.0L. Fitted with twin sequential turbochargers, it offered 325-350hp but suffered from systemic reliability issues that drove high replacement demand. Many UK-imported 2008-2010 F-550s still on the road are now on their second or third engine .
commonFailurePoints: EGR cooler failure; twin-turbo system failure; fuel injector failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford F-550 6.4L diesel engines

items.title: Ford F-550 6.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 6.0L Power Stroke V8 (2003-2007) was Navistar-built and fitted to early F-550 imports. While it offered 325hp from a 6.0L V8 configuration, it gained a notorious reputation for EGR and head gasket vulnerabilities that still drive replacement enquiries today.
commonFailurePoints: EGR cooler failure leading to head gasket failure; oil cooler clogging; high-pressure oil pump (HPOP) failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford F-550 6.0L diesel engines

items.title: Ford F-550 7.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: Ford's "Godzilla" 7.3L V8 debuted in 2020 as a brand-new pushrod petrol engine for the Super Duty range, including the F-550. Designed for simplicity, durability, and low-end torque, it produces 350hp in F-550 tuning with 468lb-ft of torque. The 7.3L uses a cast iron block with a 4.22" bore and 3.98" stroke, a 10.5:1 compression ratio, and sequential electronic fuel injection . UK operators choose the 7.3L primarily for ULEZ/CAZ compliance where diesel restrictions apply .
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump drive gear cracking (2020-2022 models
SSM 50830); lifter delamination in high-idle-hour fleet vehicles; valvetrain noise from front timing cover area
items.cta: Compare Ford F-550 7.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford F-550 6.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 6.8L Triton V10 was Ford's original Super Duty petrol engine, produced from 1999 to 2019 and fitted to early F-550 models before the 6.2L Boss was introduced. A 10-cylinder, 2-valve SOHC design, it prioritised torque over horsepower for commercial applications. The 6.8L was replaced by the 7.3L Godzilla in 2020. Some 2008-2010 F-550s with this engine remain active in UK specialist fleets .
commonFailurePoints: Exhaust manifold stud corrosion; spark plug ejection from cylinder heads (pre-2003 models); camshaft synchroniser failure
items.cta: Compare Ford F-550 6.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford F-550 6.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 6.2L Boss V8 (codenamed "Hurricane") debuted in 2011 as Ford's standard petrol engine for Super Duty trucks including the F-550. With a cast iron block, aluminium heads, and SOHC valvetrain, it produces 385hp and 405lb-ft of torque. The 6.2L was phased out from 2020 onward as the 7.3L Godzilla became the standard petrol option. UK importers value the 6.2L for its proven reliability and lower running costs compared to the diesel . Not sure which engine size your Ford F-550 needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Ford F-550 specialists.
commonFailurePoints: broken hardware (cold-start tick); rough idle from spark plugs or broken valve springs; IMRC runner flap failure causing knock-like noise
items.cta: Compare Ford F-550 6.2L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Ford F-550 needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Ford F-550 specialists.

faq.h2: Ford F-550 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Ford F-550 6.7L Power Stroke engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Expect £8,500-£15,000 for a rebuilt 6.7L Power Stroke supply-only, or £11,000-£18,000 supply and fitted. Used engines start from £4,000-£8,000 but carry higher risk.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 6.7L (SCAT/R2GA/R2GB): £8,500-£12,000 with 12-month warranty
Supply and fit adds £1,500-£3,000 depending on workshop location
Used 6.7L engines: £4,000-£8,000 with limited 30-90 day warranty
CP4 pump failure drives most replacements on 2011-2016 models
Specialist fitters save 30-50% compared to main dealer prices
items.cta: Compare Ford F-550 6.7L Power Stroke engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is the CP4 fuel pump failure on the Ford F-550 6.7L as bad as people say?
items.answer: Yes - when the CP4 pump fails, it sends metal debris through the entire fuel system, often causing £8,000-£12,000 in damage and requiring complete fuel system replacement.
keyPoints: CP4 pump failure affects 2011-2016 F-550s with 6.7L Power Stroke
Symptoms: rough idle, loss of power, metal shavings in fuel filter
COVID-linked recall covers 2020-2022 models with biodiesel deposit damage
DCR pump conversion eliminates CP4 risk permanently
Preventative CP4 disaster kits cost £600-£1,200 fitted
items.cta: Get CP4 prevention quotes for your Ford F-550 6.7L

items.question: What are the most common problems with the Ford F-550 6.2L Boss V8?
items.answer: The 6.2L Boss is generally reliable, but owners report exhaust manifold leaks (cold-start ticking), broken valve springs, oil leaks from valve covers or rear main seal, and spark plug issues.
keyPoints: Exhaust manifold bolts snap causing ticking noise that fades when warm
Broken valve springs cause rough idle and potential piston damage
Sixteen spark plugs (two per cylinder) makes replacement expensive
Oil consumption from leaking valve cover baffle (TSB 15-0161)
Many 6.2L engines exceed 200,000 miles with proper maintenance
items.cta: Compare Ford F-550 6.2L Boss V8 rebuilt engine prices

items.question: How reliable is the Ford F-550 7.3L Godzilla petrol engine?
items.answer: The 7.3L Godzilla is fundamentally durable, but extended idling causes camshaft and lifter wear due to low oil pressure at idle. Fleet trucks with high idle hours are most at risk.
keyPoints: 2020-2022 models had oil pump drive gear cracking (SSM 50830 recall)
Extended idling accelerates valvetrain wear with modern low-zinc oils
7.3L is ULEZ/CAZ compliant - no DPF, AdBlue or SCR system
Rebuilt 7.3L supply-only: £5,500-£9,500 with 12-month warranty
High-volume oil pump upgrade prevents idle-related wear
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford F-550 7.3L Godzilla replacement engines

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a high-mileage Ford F-550?
items.answer: Yes for commercial trucks with sound chassis and bodywork. A rebuilt 6.7L costs £8,500-£12,000 versus £40,000+ for a new equivalent truck - strong economic case for fleet operators.
keyPoints: New F-550 equivalent costs £60,000-£90,000+ in current market
Rebuilt engine with upgraded components outlasts original
Motorhome conversions justify replacement due to high conversion value
2011-2016 trucks are best candidates for CP4-to-DCR conversion
Specialist rebuilds include warranty - ask about 24-36 month options
items.cta: Compare Ford F-550 engine rebuild vs replacement costs

items.question: Can I fit a Ford F-550 6.7L engine in a standard UK garage?
items.answer: No - the F-550's 6.7L Power Stroke requires heavy-truck specialist equipment. You need a workshop with commercial vehicle lifts, engine hoists rated for 500kg+, and US-spec diagnostic tools.
keyPoints: Engine weight exceeds 400kg - standard garage hoists insufficient
Requires Ford IDS or J2534 passthrough programming tool
Cab must be lifted or removed on many F-550 repairs
UK network of US-spec heavy-truck specialists available through EngineMarket
Mobile fitting not recommended for complete engine replacement
items.cta: Find Ford F-550 supply and fit specialists in your UK region

items.question: What is the Ford F-550 CP4 pump recall about and does mine qualify?
items.answer: Ford recalled 295,000 Super Duty trucks (2020-2022 6.7L diesel) because biodiesel deposits from pandemic-era aged fuel accelerated CP4 pump wear. F-550s in this range receive updated programming and pump cooling improvements.
keyPoints: Recall affects 2020-2022 Ford F-550 6.7L Power Stroke models
Aged biodiesel during COVID supply disruptions caused deposit buildup
Ford updates HPFP cooling programming and inspects pump condition
Already-replaced pumps may qualify for reimbursement
Earlier 2011-2016 F-550s not covered but share same CP4 vulnerability
items.cta: Check your Ford F-550 for open recalls and get CP4 advice

items.question: Should I buy a diesel or petrol Ford F-550 for UK use?
items.answer: Diesel (6.7L) for towing and long-distance work - better fuel economy and torque. Petrol (7.3L Godzilla or 6.2L Boss) for urban operations - avoids ULEZ/CAZ charges and has no DPF/AdBlue maintenance.
keyPoints: Diesel: 475hp/1,050lb-ft torque, 15-20mpg, but DPF/SCR requires regular regens
Petrol: 350-385hp, 8-12mpg, ULEZ compliant, simpler emissions system
Petrol better for stop-start urban duty cycles
Diesel dominates motorhome and heavy towing applications
7.3L Godzilla petrol offers modern reliability with lower complexity
items.cta: Compare diesel vs petrol Ford F-550 engine prices

items.question: How long does a Ford F-550 6.7L Power Stroke engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, expect 200,000-300,000 miles from a 6.7L Power Stroke. The CP4 pump is the main threat - preventative upgrades before 120,000 miles significantly extend engine life.
keyPoints: 6.7L block and rotating assembly capable of 500,000+ miles
CP4 pump typically fails between 80,000-150,000 miles
EGR cooler lifespan: 80,000-120,000 miles before cracking risk
Regular oil changes (every 5,000-7,500 miles) critical for turbo health
DCR pump conversion extends engine life to 400,000+ miles
items.cta: Get Ford F-550 6.7L longevity upgrade quotes from specialists

items.question: What warranty applies to a Ford F-550 imported to the UK?
items.answer: Imported F-550s typically have no active Ford UK warranty unless originally sold in Europe with a transferable 2-year European manufacturer's warranty. Aftermarket warranties vary by provider.
keyPoints: US-spec imports lack Ford UK warranty coverage
European-spec imports may have 2-year transferable manufacturer warranty
Rebuilt engines through EngineMarket carry 12-month minimum warranty
Specialist-fit engines often include 24-36 month options
Verify warranty terms before purchase - especially for used engines
items.cta: Compare Ford F-550 reconditioned engine warranties from UK suppliers
